item.title: Obsession with Curry Barker
item.description:
John and Craig welcome Curry Barker, the writer-director behind this summer’s surprise blockbuster, Obsession. It’s a rom-com, a horror film and a great example of trusting your audience. We discuss Curry’s roots, his writing process, learning from YouTube, as well as the pressure to follow up a hit. In our bonus segment for premium subscribers, […]

The post Obsession with Curry Barker first appeared on John August.
item.source:markdown:
John and Craig welcome Curry Barker, the writer-director behind this summer’s surprise blockbuster, Obsession. It’s a rom-com, a horror film and a great example of trusting your audience. We discuss Curry’s roots, his writing process, learning from YouTube, as well as the pressure to follow up a hit. In our bonus segment for premium subscribers, \[…\]
The post [Obsession with Curry Barker](https://johnaugust.com/2026/obsession-with-curry-barker) first appeared on [John August](https://johnaugust.com).

item.title: Scriptnotes, Episode 740: On Competence, Transcript
item.description:
The original post for this episode can be found here. John August: Hello, and welcome. My name is John August. Craig Mazin: My name is Craig Mazin. John: This is episode 740 of Scriptnotes. It’s a podcast about screenwriting and things that are interesting to screenwriters. Today on the show, what is competence and why […]

The post Scriptnotes, Episode 740: On Competence, Transcript first appeared on John August.
item.source:markdown:
The original post for this episode can be found here. John August: Hello, and welcome. My name is John August. Craig Mazin: My name is Craig Mazin. John: This is episode 740 of Scriptnotes. It’s a podcast about screenwriting and things that are interesting to screenwriters. Today on the show, what is competence and why \[…\]
The post [Scriptnotes, Episode 740: On Competence, Transcript](https://johnaugust.com/2026/scriptnotes-episode-740-on-competence-transcript) first appeared on [John August](https://johnaugust.com).

item.title: Craig Takes Questions
item.description:
This week, Craig answers listener questions about rewriting on set, AI coverage, when and how to kill off characters, sizing up producers, and more. There’s also an extra-special guest appearance for this week’s One Cool Thing. In the bonus segment for premium subscribers, Craig discusses how to be wrong—something he feels he knows all too […]

The post Craig Takes Questions first appeared on John August.
item.source:markdown:
This week, Craig answers listener questions about rewriting on set, AI coverage, when and how to kill off characters, sizing up producers, and more. There’s also an extra-special guest appearance for this week’s One Cool Thing. In the bonus segment for premium subscribers, Craig discusses how to be wrong—something he feels he knows all too \[…\]
The post [Craig Takes Questions](https://johnaugust.com/2026/craig-takes-questions) first appeared on [John August](https://johnaugust.com).

item.title: On Competence
item.description:
Why is it so satisfying to watch a highly-skilled character complete their goals? And why can it be equally hilarious to watch an incompetent character bumble through a slew of screw-ups? In this episode, John and Craig discuss how to play with competence in your characters. We also say goodbye to our producer Drew Marquardt […]

The post On Competence first appeared on John August.
item.source:markdown:
Why is it so satisfying to watch a highly-skilled character complete their goals? And why can it be equally hilarious to watch an incompetent character bumble through a slew of screw-ups? In this episode, John and Craig discuss how to play with competence in your characters. We also say goodbye to our producer Drew Marquardt \[…\]
The post [On Competence](https://johnaugust.com/2026/on-competence) first appeared on [John August](https://johnaugust.com).

item.title: What Does “Exclusive” Mean?
item.description: When the owner of, say, a research paper assigns an exclusive license in a work to a publisher, any prior nonexclusive licenses are carved out of the exclusive license by operation of law. That does not make the exclusive license any less exclusive. The license is still a transfer of copyright ownership, the publisher still receives an exclusive right, and the publisher can exclude any future uses of that right.
item.source:markdown: When the owner of, say, a research paper assigns an exclusive license in a work to a publisher, any prior nonexclusive licenses are carved out of the exclusive license by operation of law. That does not make the exclusive license any less exclusive. The license is still a transfer of copyright ownership, the publisher still receives an exclusive right, and the publisher can exclude any future uses of that right.
